mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.3 million units signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 102.5 over 2 months signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.75% over 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 23500 over 5 months signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial jobless claims below 250000 over 2 months signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining initial jobless claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real disposable personal income above 15000 over 3 months signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
